system_role.task: Generate a production-grade Python microservice called icd10_coding_svc
system_role.description: This microservice performs inference using a frozen LLM to convert both rewritten and original clinical prompts into ICD-10 codes, and fetches ground truth codes from the dataset.
system_role.key_constraint: The service performs INFERENCE ONLY. Model weights must never be updated.

project_context.role: Frozen evaluator in a PPO reinforcement learning pipeline for clinical note ICD-10 coding
inputs: note_id — to fetch ground truth codes from dataset
project_context.fetches: gt_codes from the ground truth dataset using note_id
project_context.forwards_to: reward_metric_svc — receives enh_codes, org_codes, and gt_codes

runtime_directories.gt_codes/: Ground truth ICD-10 codes storage — one {note_id}.json per note

post_load_steps: for param in model.parameters(): param.requires_grad = False
model.constraint: The model must remain frozen and in eval mode for the entire lifetime of the service.

checkpoint_loading_logic.if_no_cache: Download from HuggingFace hub, then save to sft_checkpoints/med42/
checkpoint_loading_logic.note: The frozen LLM checkpoint never changes.

ground_truth_dataset.join_key: note_id — multiple diagnoses rows can share the same note_id, each representing one ICD-10 code
gt_fetcher_requirements: 2. Query diagnoses table for all rows matching note_id
3. Return the list of icd_code values as gt_codes
ground_truth_dataset.loading: notes.csv and diagnoses.csv are loaded once at service startup from paths defined in config.py

disk_persistence.cache_first: If gt_codes/{note_id}.json already exists on disk, load from disk without querying the dataset.

request_example.original_prompt: Extract ICD-10 codes from: Patient has chest pain...
request_example.rewritten_prompt: Step 1: Identify symptoms... Step 2: Map to ICD-10...

fields.note_id: Used to fetch gt_codes from dataset
fields.original_prompt: Unmodified prompt, passed directly to model
fields.rewritten_prompt: Enhanced prompt from Prompt Rewriter Service

inference_workflow.shared_config: Both passes use identical generation config and the same frozen model instance
inference_workflow.grad_context: Both passes must run inside torch.no_grad()
inference_workflow.result: All three (enh_codes, org_codes, gt_codes) are included in the response and forwarded to reward_metric_svc

generation_config.system_instruction: You are a clinical coding expert. Extract all ICD-10-CM codes from the given clinical note. Output codes as a JSON list only. Do not include explanations.
generation_config.system_instruction_note: The same system instruction is used for both inference passes. Defined in config.py.

strategies.strategy_1: JSON extraction — parse JSON list directly from model output
strategies.strategy_2: Regex extraction — apply regex pattern if JSON parsing fails
strategies.strategy_3: Fallback — return empty list, set parsing_success = false, log warning

field_definitions.gt_codes: Ground truth codes fetched via note_id
field_definitions.enh_raw_output: Raw model text for enhanced pass (for debugging)
field_definitions.org_raw_output: Raw model text for original pass (for debugging)
field_definitions.parsing_success: false if either parsing pass returns empty list

downstream_forwarding.async: Forwarding must happen asynchronously after the API response is returned — must not block response latency
downstream_forwarding.on_failure: Log the error and retry once. Do not raise an exception to the caller.

functions: parse_icd10_codes(raw_output: str) → list[str] — JSON → regex → fallback strategy

schemas_py.CodeRequest: { note_id: str, original_prompt: str, rewritten_prompt: str }
schemas_py.CodeResponse: { note_id: str, enh_codes: list[str], org_codes: list[str], gt_codes: list[str], enh_raw_output: str, org_raw_output: str, parsing_success: bool }

log_events: Forwarding to reward service (success or failure)

concurrency.reason: Prevent CUDA OOM errors from concurrent GPU calls
concurrency.scope: Each request triggers two sequential inference passes within a single lock acquisition to ensure atomicity

service_overview.name: icd10_coding_svc
service_overview.purpose: Frozen LLM inference microservice that converts clinical prompts into ICD-10 codes and fetches ground truth codes. Acts as the evaluator in a PPO RL pipeline for clinical note coding.
service_overview.key_constraint: Inference ONLY — model weights must never be updated. Model always stays frozen and in eval() mode.

runtime_directories.gt_codes/: Persisted ground truth JSON files, one per note_id. Located at project root (../gt_codes/).
runtime_directories.inference_outputs/icd10/: Persisted inference result JSON files per request.
runtime_directories.sft_checkpoints/med42/: Local model cache. Load from here if present; otherwise download from HuggingFace and save here.
runtime_directories.data/: Contains notes.csv and diagnoses.csv loaded at startup.

quantization.reason: Required to fit model on T4 16GB GPU

post_load_steps: for param in model.parameters(): param.requires_grad = False

checkpoint_logic.if_no_cache: Download from HuggingFace using hub_id, then save_pretrained to LOCAL_CACHE_PATH

model.caching: model_loader.py must cache the loaded (model, tokenizer) instance in memory so it is only loaded once at service startup.

constants.SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION: You are a clinical coding expert. Extract all ICD-10-CM codes from the given clinical note. Output codes as a JSON list only. Do not include explanations.

CodeRequest.note_id: str — identifies the clinical note; used to fetch gt_codes
CodeRequest.original_prompt: str — unmodified clinical prompt passed directly to model
CodeRequest.rewritten_prompt: str — enhanced prompt produced by Prompt Rewriter Service

CodeResponse.enh_codes: list[str] — ICD-10 codes extracted from rewritten_prompt inference
CodeResponse.org_codes: list[str] — ICD-10 codes extracted from original_prompt inference
CodeResponse.gt_codes: list[str] — ground truth codes fetched via note_id
CodeResponse.enh_raw_output: str — raw model text for enhanced pass (debug)
CodeResponse.org_raw_output: str — raw model text for original pass (debug)
CodeResponse.parsing_success: bool — false if either pass returns an empty code list

schemas_py.implementation_note: Use Pydantic BaseModel for both. Import in app.py and inference_engine.py.

inference_engine_py.public_function: run_inference(note_id: str, original_prompt: str, rewritten_prompt: str) -> dict
steps: 3. Build Llama-3 chat-formatted prompt string for rewritten_prompt
4. Tokenize and run model.generate() inside torch.no_grad() → enh_raw_output
5. Build Llama-3 chat-formatted prompt string for original_prompt
6. Tokenize and run model.generate() inside torch.no_grad() → org_raw_output
13. Schedule async forwarding to reward_metric_svc (BackgroundTasks or threading.Thread)

concurrency.scope: Both inference passes (enh and org) run within a single lock acquisition
concurrency.reason: Prevents CUDA OOM from concurrent GPU calls

logic: 1. Check if GT_CODES_PATH/{note_id}.json exists on disk
2. If YES: load and return gt_codes list from JSON file (skip CSV query)
3. If NO: filter diagnoses DataFrame (loaded at startup) for rows where note_id matches
4. Collect all icd_code values from matching rows into a list

dataset.notes_csv: data/notes.csv — columns: note_id, subject_id_x, hadm_id, text
dataset.loading: Both CSVs are loaded once at service startup via pandas.read_csv(). Store as module-level DataFrames.
dataset.join_key: note_id (string — treat as str to avoid type mismatch)

parse_icd10_codes.strategy_1_json: Attempt json.loads() on the raw output or the first JSON array found via regex r'\[.*?\]' with re.DOTALL. If successful and result is a list, validate each element with validate_code().
parse_icd10_codes.strategy_2_regex: If JSON fails, apply re.findall(ICD10_REGEX_PATTERN, raw_output) to extract all matching code strings.
parse_icd10_codes.strategy_3_fallback: If regex also returns empty list, log a warning, set parsing_success indicator to false, return [].
parse_icd10_codes.deduplication: Always return list(dict.fromkeys(codes)) to preserve order while deduplicating.

validate_code.rule: Return True if re.fullmatch(ICD10_REGEX_PATTERN, code) is not None, else False.

log_events: Model loaded — include model name and source (cache or HuggingFace)
Enhanced inference completed — include elapsed time in seconds
Original inference completed — include elapsed time in seconds
gt_codes fetched — include note_id and source (disk or dataset) and count
Codes parsed — include strategy used (json/regex/fallback) and count for each pass
Forwarding to reward_metric_svc — include success/failure and note_id

app_py.startup_event: Load model (model_loader.load_model()), load notes.csv and diagnoses.csv DataFrames into gt_fetcher module, create output directories if not present.

logic: 1. Validate request via Pydantic (automatic with FastAPI)
4. After response is returned, fire async background task to POST to reward_metric_svc

downstream_forwarding.async: Use FastAPI BackgroundTasks or a daemon threading.Thread after response is sent
downstream_forwarding.retry: On failure, retry once. If second attempt also fails, log error only — do not raise exception to caller.

testing.overview: Tests are split into three tiers: unit (mock model/GPU), integration (real module wiring, mock HTTP), e2e (full service running).

cases.name: test_parse_json_success
cases.description: Raw output is a valid JSON list of ICD-10 codes. Expect parse_icd10_codes to return the correct deduplicated list using strategy 1.

cases.name: test_parse_regex_fallback
cases.description: Raw output is prose containing ICD-10 codes but not valid JSON. Expect strategy 2 regex extraction to succeed.
cases.input: 'The patient has R07.9 and also I20.9.'

cases.name: test_parse_empty_fallback
cases.description: Raw output contains no ICD-10 codes. Expect strategy 3 fallback to return empty list.

cases.name: test_validate_code_valid
cases.description: Pass a well-formed ICD-10 code. validate_code should return True.

cases.name: test_validate_code_invalid
cases.description: Pass a malformed string. validate_code should return False.

cases.name: test_parse_deduplication
cases.description: JSON list contains duplicate codes. Expect output list to have each code only once, order preserved.

test_gt_fetcher.py.setup: Use tmp_path pytest fixture to create temporary gt_codes/ dir and sample diagnoses CSV. Monkey-patch gt_fetcher module-level DataFrames.

cases.name: test_get_gt_codes_from_disk
cases.description: A {note_id}.json already exists in gt_codes/. Expect get_gt_codes to return the list from disk without reading the DataFrame.
cases.mock: Pre-create gt_codes/12345.json with known codes. Patch diagnoses_df so test would fail if it's accessed.

cases.name: test_get_gt_codes_from_dataset
cases.description: No cached file exists. DataFrame has matching rows for the note_id. Expect codes to be returned and a new JSON file written to disk.
cases.mock: Use a small pandas DataFrame with 3 rows for note_id=12345.
cases.expected: Returns correct list; gt_codes/12345.json is created with correct content.

cases.name: test_get_gt_codes_unknown_note
cases.description: No disk file and no matching rows in DataFrame. Expect empty list and an empty gt_codes file written.

test_inference_engine.py.setup: Mock model_loader.load_model() to return (mock_model, mock_tokenizer). Mock torch.no_grad(). Mock gt_fetcher.get_gt_codes(). Mock file I/O. Do NOT require a GPU.

cases.name: test_run_inference_returns_correct_keys
cases.description: Call run_inference with valid inputs using mocked model. Expect result dict to contain all required keys: note_id, enh_codes, org_codes, gt_codes, enh_raw_output, org_raw_output, parsing_success.
cases.mock: model.generate() returns mock token tensor. tokenizer.decode() returns a JSON string of codes.

cases.name: test_run_inference_parsing_success_true
cases.description: mock model output returns valid ICD-10 JSON for both passes. Expect parsing_success = True.

cases.name: test_run_inference_parsing_success_false
cases.description: Mock model output returns empty string for one pass. Expect parsing_success = False.

cases.name: test_output_file_written
cases.description: After run_inference, verify that a JSON file is created in OUTPUT_PATH with the correct fields.

cases.name: test_inference_lock_acquired
cases.description: Patch threading.Lock and verify it is acquired and released exactly once per call to run_inference.

test_service_pipeline.py.setup: Use FastAPI TestClient. Mock model_loader.load_model() and gt_fetcher calls. Mock httpx POST to reward_metric_svc.

cases.name: test_post_generate_codes_valid_request
cases.description: POST valid CodeRequest to /generate_codes. Expect 200 and a response body conforming to CodeResponse schema with all required fields.
cases.mock: inference_engine.run_inference patched to return a known result dict.

cases.name: test_post_generate_codes_missing_field
cases.description: POST request body missing rewritten_prompt. Expect 422 Unprocessable Entity from Pydantic validation.

cases.name: test_get_health
cases.description: GET /health. Expect 200 and JSON with weights_frozen: true and model name.

cases.name: test_forwarding_called_after_response
cases.description: After POST /generate_codes, verify that the background task fires a POST to REWARD_SERVICE_URL/compute_reward with the correct payload fields.
cases.mock: Patch httpx.AsyncClient.post or requests.post. Inspect call args.

cases.name: test_forwarding_failure_does_not_propagate
cases.description: Mock httpx to raise an exception on the first call and succeed on the retry. Verify the /generate_codes response is still 200 and no exception is raised.

test_end_to_end_inference.py.prerequisite: Requires the service to be running locally on port 8001 with a real or stubbed model. Skip if GPU not available using pytest.mark.skipif.

cases.name: test_full_inference_cycle
cases.description: Send a real POST request to http://localhost:8001/generate_codes with a sample clinical note_id. Verify the response has non-empty enh_codes or org_codes and parsing_success is either True or False (no crash).
cases.note: Use a real note_id that exists in data/diagnoses.csv.

cases.name: test_gt_codes_persisted_to_disk
cases.description: After the above call, verify that gt_codes/{note_id}.json exists on disk and contains a gt_codes list.
cases.expected: File exists; gt_codes is a list.

cases.name: test_inference_output_persisted_to_disk
cases.description: After a successful call, verify that at least one file exists in inference_outputs/icd10/ matching the pattern {note_id}_*.json with the correct schema fields.
cases.expected: File exists; contains timestamp, enh_codes, org_codes, gt_codes.

cases.name: test_health_endpoint_live
cases.description: GET http://localhost:8001/health. Expect 200 and weights_frozen: true.

pytest_configuration.skip_gpu_marker: Use @pytest.mark.skipif(not torch.cuda.is_available(), reason='GPU required') on e2e tests and any test that cannot be fully mocked.
recommended_fixtures: tmp_path — for temporary file I/O in gt_fetcher and output storage tests
monkeypatch — for replacing module-level globals (DataFrames, model instances)
unittest.mock.patch — for mocking model.generate(), httpx calls, threading.Lock
